title: Early land animals skipped the tadpole phase
gist: A new Science study of embolomere fossils overturns the long-held assumption that early tetrapods passed through an aquatic tadpole stage, showing they developed directly like reptiles; amphibian metamorphosis may be a later innovation rather than an ancient stepping stone to land.

insights.claim: A 50-60-year-old Field Museum fossil (FMNH PR 1082), re-examined with electron microscopy, was identified as a newly hatched embolomere that looked like a miniature adult, had an internalized abdominal yolk, ossified bones, and no external gills.
insights.speaker: Jacek Krywko
insights.so_what: Demonstrates how modern imaging applied to legacy museum collections can overturn decades-old taxonomic and developmental assumptions.
insights.locator: One of the most important specimens

insights.claim: The internalized yolk mass in the hatchling suggests the ancestral tetrapod egg was large and nutrient-dense like reptile and bird eggs, not the small eggs of amphibians and fish.
insights.speaker: Jacek Krywko
insights.so_what: Reframes the evolutionary origin of amniote-style reproduction earlier than previously assumed.
insights.locator: The team also realized that this young embolomere

insights.claim: Across multiple lineages including embolomers, the limbless Phlegethontia longissima, and megalichthyids predating embolomers by 20-30 million years, no specimen showed a tadpole-like larval stage.
insights.speaker: Jason Pardo
insights.so_what: Broad cross-lineage sampling makes the no-tadpole finding a general evolutionary pattern rather than an outlier.
insights.quote: none of them have anything that looks remotely like a tadpole
insights.locator: We looked at a number of different species

insights.claim: Amphibian metamorphosis may be a later evolutionary innovation unique to amphibians rather than an ancient stepping stone that enabled the first water-to-land transition.
insights.speaker: Jason Pardo
insights.so_what: Inverts a textbook narrative, illustrating how foundational assumptions in a field can rest on limited data.
insights.quote: Instead of being primitive, it may actually be something new, something novel and exciting.
insights.locator: He suggested that this is evidence

insights.claim: Direct development likely made the water-to-land transition harder, as juvenile embolomers with weak limbs competed in the same niche as three-meter adults and were stuck where they hatched.
insights.speaker: Jacek Krywko
insights.so_what: Challenges the intuition that a larval stage was necessary for terrestrialization.
